issues.current_text: the `x` value widened to the target numeric type
issues.problem: The phrasing `the "x" value` is slightly awkward; since this single doc comment serves five different implicit conversions (byte2short, byte2int, byte2long, byte2float, byte2double), naming the target type generically is correct, but the wording could read a bit more naturally.
issues.suggestion: the value of `x` widened to the target numeric type

general_feedback: The only change in this file is filling in the @return tag for the shared documentation comment covering the five `byte2*` implicit widening conversions in the companion object. The new text accurately describes the behavior (each method calls `.toShort`/`.toInt`/`.toLong`/`.toFloat`/`.toDouble` on `x`), starts with a lowercase letter, uses backticks for the code reference, and is appropriately concise. It pairs well with the existing @param `x the "Byte" value to convert`. Only a minor stylistic suggestion; no factual issues.
